query:
so there are alot of interesting details in this design... I'm asking you to review the proposed design and provide me your thoughts product/features/crt-033 you'll find architecture/architecture.md and specification/specification.md.  These are the 3 things highlighted, but I'm a little suspect of this: Three items for your review before delivery proceeds:           
                                                                  
  1. pending_cycle_reviews source — SCOPE said                    
  query_log.feature_cycle (column doesn't exist). Architecture and
   spec both converged on cycle_events with                       
  event_type='cycle_start'. This changes semantics slightly:    
  "cycle had a start event" vs "Unimatrix was queried during this
  cycle." Confirm the substitution matches intent.
  2. Schema cascade touchpoint count — architecture lists 7
  touchpoints, spec AC-02b lists 5. Architecture is authoritative 
  for delivery. AC-02b in the spec could be updated before
  delivery starts if you want the spec to be the single source.   
  3. raw_signals_available type — spec says bool, architecture  
  struct says i32. Delivery must resolve before implementation    
  (sqlx binding differs).

entries.title: ADR-001 col-022: Reuse RecordEvent for Cycle Lifecycle Wire Protocol
ADR-003 col-024: Structured Log Event on Primary-Path Fallback Activation
ADR-002 crt-033: Unified SUMMARY_SCHEMA_VERSION const in cycle_review_index.rs
cycle_events-first observation lookup: session identification via topic_signal + time windows
Design agents following an established pattern should query the pattern entry, not re-read source files
